pregnancy.message: Pregnancy exposure can increase fetal risk; use only with specialist review.

breastfeeding.message: Lithium passes into breast milk; infant toxicity monitoring is required.

condition_rules.message: Renal impairment can raise lithium levels and toxicity risk.
Volume and sodium shifts can increase lithium concentration variability.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: CKD stage G3+ increases risk for lithium accumulation; dose and monitoring adjustment required.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Reduced CrCl increases lithium exposure and toxicity risk.

interaction_rules.message: ACEi and ARB therapy can increase lithium concentrations.
NSAIDs can increase lithium concentrations.
Diuretics can increase lithium concentrations and toxicity risk.

pregnancy.message: Avoid in pregnancy when possible due to major fetal risk.

breastfeeding.message: Generally compatible with monitoring; assess infant sedation and liver effects.

condition_rules.message: Valproate is contraindicated or high risk in significant hepatic disease.
Valproate may precipitate severe adverse effects in these conditions.

albumin_below_g_dl.message: Low albumin can increase free valproate fraction and adverse effects.

interaction_rules.message: Valproate increases lamotrigine exposure and severe rash risk.
Enzyme-inducing antiepileptics can alter valproate exposure.
Combined use can increase bleeding risk; monitor closely.

pregnancy.message: Use in pregnancy requires risk-benefit review and close monitoring.

breastfeeding.message: Generally compatible with monitoring for infant sedation.

condition_rules.message: Hepatic impairment can increase phenytoin exposure.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: Advanced CKD may increase free phenytoin fraction; monitor free levels when possible.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Reduced renal function can alter free phenytoin concentrations.

albumin_below_g_dl.message: Low albumin increases free phenytoin fraction and toxicity risk.

interaction_rules.message: Valproate can displace protein binding and increase free phenytoin.
Combined antiepileptics can produce complex PK interactions.
Interaction may alter phenytoin exposure or anticoagulation response.

pregnancy.message: Use in pregnancy requires risk-benefit assessment and specialist follow-up.

breastfeeding.message: May be compatible with breastfeeding with infant monitoring.

condition_rules.message: Hepatic impairment increases risk of adverse effects.
May worsen cardiac conduction abnormalities.

interaction_rules.message: These agents can increase carbamazepine concentrations.
Co-administered antiepileptics may alter exposure.
Enzyme induction may reduce anticoagulant effect.

pregnancy.message: Use cautiously and monitor concentrations in pregnancy.

breastfeeding.message: Monitor infant for irritability and feeding changes.

condition_rules.message: These conditions can reduce theophylline clearance.

interaction_rules.message: May raise theophylline levels and toxicity risk.
Enzyme induction may lower theophylline concentrations.

pregnancy.message: Use with close maternal monitoring when clinically indicated.

condition_rules.message: Underlying conduction disease can increase digoxin adverse effects.
Electrolyte disturbances increase digoxin toxicity risk.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: Renal impairment increases digoxin exposure; consider dose adjustment.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Low CrCl is associated with higher digoxin concentrations.

interaction_rules.message: Can increase digoxin exposure or conduction-related adverse effects.
Macrolides may increase digoxin concentrations.
Diuretics can trigger electrolyte changes that increase toxicity risk.

pregnancy.message: Use in pregnancy should be individualized; monitor maternal risk factors.

breastfeeding.message: Generally low risk in breastfeeding with routine monitoring.

condition_rules.message: Baseline cardiac or electrolyte risk can increase QT-related adverse event risk.

interaction_rules.message: Combined QT-prolonging therapy can increase arrhythmia risk.

pregnancy.message: Use if clinically necessary with renal monitoring.

breastfeeding.message: Low oral absorption in infant; monitor for GI effects.

condition_rules.message: Renal dysfunction increases vancomycin exposure and nephrotoxicity risk.
Baseline hearing concerns warrant closer toxicity monitoring.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: CKD stage G3+ requires careful dosing and exposure monitoring.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Low CrCl raises accumulation and AKI risk.

interaction_rules.message: Combined nephrotoxic and ototoxic burden is increased.
Concurrent nephrotoxic agents can increase renal injury risk.

pregnancy.message: Aminoglycosides in pregnancy require specialist oversight.

breastfeeding.message: Monitor infant GI tolerance and fungal overgrowth signs.

condition_rules.message: Renal stress increases aminoglycoside toxicity risk.
Neuromuscular and auditory toxicity risk is increased.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: CKD stage G3+ requires close exposure monitoring.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Low CrCl increases gentamicin accumulation risk.

interaction_rules.message: Concurrent nephrotoxic or ototoxic therapy increases adverse event risk.

pregnancy.message: Aminoglycosides in pregnancy require specialist oversight.

breastfeeding.message: Monitor infant GI tolerance and fungal overgrowth signs.

condition_rules.message: Renal stress increases aminoglycoside toxicity risk.
Neuromuscular and auditory toxicity risk is increased.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: CKD stage G3+ requires close exposure monitoring.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Low CrCl increases tobramycin accumulation risk.

interaction_rules.message: Concurrent nephrotoxic or ototoxic therapy increases adverse event risk.

pregnancy.message: Aminoglycosides in pregnancy require specialist oversight.

breastfeeding.message: Monitor infant GI tolerance and fungal overgrowth signs.

condition_rules.message: Renal stress increases aminoglycoside toxicity risk.
Neuromuscular and auditory toxicity risk is increased.

ckd_stage_at_least.message: CKD stage G3+ requires close exposure monitoring.

creatinine_clearance_below_ml_min.message: Low CrCl increases amikacin accumulation risk.

interaction_rules.message: Concurrent nephrotoxic or ototoxic therapy increases adverse event risk.

pregnancy.message: Use in pregnancy requires specialist oversight and maternal-fetal monitoring.

breastfeeding.message: Monitor infant for sedation and poor feeding.

condition_rules.message: Hepatic impairment can reduce clearance and increase sedation risk.
May worsen respiratory depression or precipitate porphyria symptoms.

interaction_rules.message: Combined antiepileptics can alter exposure and CNS adverse effects.
Enzyme induction may reduce anticoagulant effect.
